situation: audit-cycle-state was cycle-complete after wanxia-edit drift-repair. Triggered new full audit cycle. Selected autonomous-engine (sister project to autonomous-studio, never deep-audited). IS_GIT=true so opt-worktree dispatch viable.
action: Deep audit of all 7 hook/watchdog scripts in .claude/hooks/ + .claude/watchdog.sh. Read ARCHITECTURE.md for context. Identified 10 findings (2H/5M/2L/1I). Wrote audit report audit-2026-07-06-024.md. Updated audit-cycle-state.json with 9 derived_fixes (all pending). Status transitioned to fix-in-progress. No opt-worktree commit this round (audit round produces report only; fixes dispatched next rounds).
outcome_evidence: audit-2026-07-06-024.md created (10 findings documented with file:line references). audit-cycle-state.json updated: status=fix-in-progress, pending_count=9, current_audit_id=audit-2026-07-06-024. History entry appended.
next_suggestion: Next round: dispatch first derived_fix from audit-2026-07-06-024. Recommend H-002 (save-checkpoint.py non-atomic write) as highest-impact fix — data integrity on resume path. Use opt-worktree.sh against /home/admin/workspace/autonomous-engine (not autonomous-studio). After H-002 merged, proceed H-001 → M-001 → M-004/M-005 (batch x-tool path fixes).

audit_findings.finding: Path referenced before import — NameError at runtime when find_task_files() called. Import is inside if __name__=='__main__' block (line 215), unreachable in hook invocation context.
audit_findings.remediation: Move 'from pathlib import Path' to top-level imports (after line 17), or delete unused find_task_files() function entirely.

audit_findings.finding: Checkpoint write is non-atomic: json.dump directly to target path + shutil.copy2 for latest.json. Process crash mid-write leaves corrupt JSON; concurrent watchdog read gets partial content. Contrast with incremental-save.py:44-58 which already uses correct tempfile+fsync+os.replace pattern.
audit_findings.remediation: Adopt incremental-save.py atomic write pattern: tempfile.mkstemp in same dir → json.dump → flush/fsync → os.replace for both checkpoint file and LATEST_LINK.

audit_findings.finding: subprocess.run passes env={**os.environ, ...} leaking all parent environment variables (including potential API keys/tokens) to bootstrap.py child process. Defense-in-depth gap.
audit_findings.remediation: Construct minimal env dict with only PATH, PYTHONPATH, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, HOME, LANG.

audit_findings.finding: Session context file read-modify-write without locking. Rapid consecutive events within same session can lose field updates via last-writer-wins race.
audit_findings.remediation: Add fcntl.flock around read-modify-write, or document eventual-consistency trade-off in comments.

audit_findings.finding: TCP payload has no length prefix and uses unescaped | delimiter. Termux listener recv() may split at arbitrary byte boundaries; title/message containing | breaks parsing.
audit_findings.remediation: Add 4-byte big-endian length prefix, or switch to newline-terminated protocol with escaping.

audit_findings.finding: PROJECT_DIR hardcoded to /mnt/e/x-tool — copy-paste artifact from x-tool project. Deploying this watchdog for autonomous-engine monitors wrong directory.
audit_findings.remediation: Derive from script location: PROJECT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)" or read from $AUTONOMOUS_ENGINE_DIR env var.

audit_findings.finding: Engine firmware template hardcodes E:/x-tool/ Windows paths (lines 121-122). Injected into Claude system prompt, misdirects agent to nonexistent paths on non-Windows/non-x-tool deployments.
audit_findings.remediation: Use f-string with PROJECT_DIR variable (already defined at line 25) to replace hardcoded paths.

audit_findings.finding: Multiple bare except clauses silently swallow exceptions (safe_append_jsonl:55, write_session_context:184, state file write:365). Correct design intent (don't block main flow) but zero observability for debugging.
audit_findings.remediation: Add stderr debug logging with file/line context in safe_append_jsonl and write_session_context except blocks.

audit_findings.finding: run() helper uses shell=True with string commands. Currently safe (all cmds are hardcoded literals) but maintenance hazard — future parameterization could introduce shell injection.
